answer:
**In *San Hyan Kamahayanikan* (ch. 1), the Five Tathāgatas are introduced with their characteristic wisdoms in the following passage:**[^1]

> Here is the order of the wisdom of the Five Tathāgatas in the Sacred Mahāyāna.  
> *ŚĀŚVATAJÑĀNABUDDHAŚ CA ADARŚAJÑĀNĀKṢOBHYAŚ CA*  
> *SAMATA RATNASAMBHAVAḤ KṚTYAÑ CĀMOGHASIDDHIDAḤ*  
> *PRATYAVEKṢAṆA VIJÑEYO LOKEŚVARA PARAṀSUKHAṀ*  
> *ETĀNI PAÑCAJÑĀNĀNI GUHYAÑ CA PARIKĪRTYATE.*

The commentary explains them in order (Vairocana, Akṣobhya, Ratnasambhava, Amitābha, Amoghasiddhi):

> The non-conceptual wisdom in which there is grasping to a self is called eternal wisdom (*śāśvatajñāna*). That is the wisdom of Lord Vairocana.  
> The luminous wisdom, wisdom that shines like the splendor of the sun, is called **mirror-like wisdom (*ādarśajñāna*)**. **That is the wisdom of Lord Akṣobhya**.[^2]  
> The wisdom that is free from perceiver and perceived... is called the wisdom of the equality of space (*ākāśamatajñāna*). That is the wisdom of Lord Ratnasambhava.  
> ...[continues similarly for the rest]. Such are the distinctions of the Five Wisdoms for the lord of yogis. They are a supreme secret.

**Akṣobhya (the second) is described elsewhere in the chapter as well—for instance, as consort to the Divine Lady Locanā** (with their essences unified as one),[^3] **with seed-syllable *hūṁ*,**[^4] **embodying aversion (*dveṣa*) among the three poisons,**[^5] **consciousness (*vijñāna*) among the five aggregates,**[^6] **the space element (*ākāśadhātu*),**[^7] **and mind (*citta*) among the three bodies (*trikāya*).**[^8]

[^1]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-36988a).
[^2]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-ff7d3d).
[^3]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-53bcda).
[^4]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-358765).
[^5]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-f4b362).
[^6]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-7298d4).
[^7]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-cee005).
[^8]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-39287a).
